(German pronunciation: [[ˈhaːdɐn]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:De-Hadern.ogg "File:De-Hadern.ogg")) is the 20th borough of the [Bavarian](/wiki/Bavaria "Bavaria") city of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich") in [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany").

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [48°06′38″N 11°27′55″E﻿ / ﻿48.11056°N 11.46528°E﻿ / 48.11056; 11.46528](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Hadern&params=48_06_38_N_11_27_55_E_region:DE_type:landmark_source:dewiki)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Borough of Munich, Germany

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/40/M%C3%BCnchen_-_Stadtbezirk_20_%28Karte%29_-_Hadern.png/250px-M%C3%BCnchen_-_Stadtbezirk_20_%28Karte%29_-_Hadern.png)](/wiki/File:M%C3%BCnchen_-_Stadtbezirk_20_(Karte)_-_Hadern.png)

Borough 20 **Hadern**, Location in Munich

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/64/Muenchen_Klinikum_Grosshadern.jpg/250px-Muenchen_Klinikum_Grosshadern.jpg)](/wiki/File:Muenchen_Klinikum_Grosshadern.jpg)

The [Klinikum Großhadern](/wiki/Klinikum_Gro%C3%9Fhadern "Klinikum Großhadern")

**Hadern** (German pronunciation: [[ˈhaːdɐn]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:De-Hadern.ogg "File:De-Hadern.ogg")) is the 20th borough of the [Bavarian](/wiki/Bavaria "Bavaria") city of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich") in [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany").

## Location

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hadern&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Location")]

Located in the south-west of Munich, Hadern's borders are the Autobahn Munich-Garmish in the south, *Fürstenriederstrasse* in the east and *Senftenauerstrasse* in the north and also the south. A large part of the borough is occupied by the [Waldfriedhof](/wiki/Munich_Waldfriedhof "Munich Waldfriedhof"), the largest cemetery in the city.

## History and description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hadern&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History and description")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This section **does not [cite](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") any [sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this section](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Hadern "Special:EditPage/Hadern") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and [removed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Burden_of_evidence "Wikipedia:Verifiability"). *(May 2011)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

Hadern was originally a farmer village and was first mentioned in the 11th century by the name of Haderun. [Großhadern](/wiki/Gro%C3%9Fhadern "Großhadern") belonged to the [Benediktbeuern Abbey](/wiki/Benediktbeuern_Abbey "Benediktbeuern Abbey"), Kleinhadern to the [Schäftlarn Abbey](/wiki/Sch%C3%A4ftlarn_Abbey "Schäftlarn Abbey"). Großhadern was incorporated into Munich in 1938, thus making it the youngest district. The structures of the former rural settlement in Großhadern can still clearly be made out in the old village centre. Structurally, Hadern consists mainly of family homes. On areas previously used for agricultural purposes in the district, large living complexes like the *Blumenau* or the complexes on *Senftenauerstrasse* and in [Neuhadern](/w/index.php?title=Neuhadern&action=edit&redlink=1 "Neuhadern (page does not exist)") with a high proportion of council tenants were built after the Second World War until into the 80s.

New residential buildings are currently being built south of the [Klinikum Großhadern](/wiki/Klinikum_Gro%C3%9Fhadern "Klinikum Großhadern"). Since the extension of [U-Bahn](/wiki/Munich_U-Bahn "Munich U-Bahn") [Line 6](/wiki/Munich_U-Bahn#U6 "Munich U-Bahn") to the clinic, the district is well connected in terms of public transport.

The Klinikum Großhadern (Großhadern Medical Centre) of [LMU Munich](/wiki/LMU_Munich "LMU Munich") is the largest hospital complex in Munich. In 1994 the [Genzentrum](/w/index.php?title=Genzentrum&action=edit&redlink=1 "Genzentrum (page does not exist)") (Gene Centre) of the LMU was put into operation and in 1999 the entire chemical/pharmaceutical faculty was relocated to Hadern. Further constructional development followed in 2004 with the inclusion of the lecture and research department in *Department Biologie II* of the *Biozentrums der HighTech CampusLMU* (Biology Centre of the HighTech CampusLMU). Over the mid and long term the entire biological faculty and further science faculties and institutes are to be consolidated together here.

The *Kinderzentrum des Bezirks Oberbayern* (Children's centre of [Oberbayern](/wiki/Oberbayern "Oberbayern")) and the associated Montessorischule as well as the Augustinum elderly home[[1]](#cite_note-1) in the area and its associated Stiftsklinik are further social facilities located in the district, that along with the clinical centre, constitute the majority of available jobs in the area.

The social order of the population consists of young people, workers as well as elderly people, above all in residential buildings and homes for one family. The proportion of foreigners in Hadern is under the city average.
